mainTopic: (continues) אין מערבין פירות בפירות · פרקוס ואונאה
overview: (1) The Mishnah and Gemara discussing the prohibition of mixing new and old produce, and the specific laws regarding mixing wine, dregs, and water. (2) The Mishnah and Gemara regarding the prohibition of 'Perkus' (deceptive practices like giving nuts to children or soaking grain) and the definitions of these terms. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Mishnah and Gemara discussing the prohibition of mixing new and old produce, and the specific laws regarding mixing wine, dregs, and water.

The Mishnah and Gemara regarding the prohibition of 'Perkus' (deceptive practices like giving nuts to children or soaking grain) and the definitions of these terms. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: The exception for wine and the rule of 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ'
steps.stepSummary: The Mishnah permits mixing strong and weak wine because it improves it, and Rabbi Elazar rules that 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ' indicates a settled halakha.
steps.whatsHappening: The Mishnah introduces an exception for wine, and Rabbi Elazar makes a general rule about the phrase 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ'.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Mishnah shifts to wine, permitting mixing strong and weak wine because it enhances the product. It then prohibits mixing dregs with wine, but allows selling wine with its own dregs. Rabbi Elazar then makes a sweeping statement: whenever the Mishnah uses the phrase 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ', it indicates a settled halakha without dispute.

keyTerms.meaning: truthfully they said / actually they said

steps.whatToRemember: The phrase 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ' signals a settled halakha.
steps.confusionAlert: The Mishnah's permission to mix wine seems to contradict the general prohibition on mixing produce. The Gemara will need to resolve this.
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes the exception for wine and introduces a key hermeneutical principle.

phrases.aramaic: בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ: בְּיַיִן הִתִּירוּ לְעָרֵב קָשֶׁה בְּרַךְ,
phrases.english: Truthfully they said: In wine they permitted to mix hard in soft,
Because it improves it.
One does not mix wine dregs into wine,
But one gives him its dregs.
One whose wine had water mixed into it,
May not sell it in a store,

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא אִם כֵּן הוֹדִיעוֹ. וְלֹא לַתַּגָּר,
phrases.english: Unless he informed him. And not to a wholesaler,
Even though he informed him,
Because he is only to cheat with it.

phrases.aramaic: מָקוֹם שֶׁנָּהֲגוּ לְהָטִיל מַיִם בַּיַּיִן – יָטִילוּ.
phrases.english: A place where they are accustomed to throw water into the wine – they throw.

phrases.aramaic: הַתַּגָּר נוֹטֵל מֵחָמֵשׁ גֳּרָנוֹת וְנוֹתֵן לְתוֹךְ מְגוּרָה אַחַת,
phrases.english: The wholesaler takes from five barns and gives into one vat,

phrases.aramaic: מֵחָמֵשׁ גִּתּוֹת וְנוֹתֵן לְתוֹךְ פִּיטוֹם אֶחָד,
phrases.english: From five presses and gives into one barrel,
And only that he not intend to mix.
Gemara: The rabbis taught:

phrases.aramaic: אֵין צָרִיךְ לוֹמַר חֲדָשׁוֹת מֵאַרְבַּע וִישָׁנוֹת
phrases.english: It is not necessary to say new ones from four and old ones
From three that one does not mix,

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא אֲפִילּוּ חֲדָשׁוֹת מִשָּׁלֹשׁ וִישָׁנוֹת מֵאַרְבַּע –
phrases.english: But even new ones from three and old ones from four –

phrases.aramaic: אֵין מְעָרְבִין, מִפְּנֵי שֶׁאָדָם רוֹצֶה לְיַשְּׁנָן.
phrases.english: One does not mix, because a person wants to age them.

phrases.aramaic: בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ: בַּיַּיִן הִתִּירוּ לְעָרֵב קָשֶׁה בְּרַךְ,
phrases.english: Truthfully they said: In wine they permitted to mix hard in soft,
Because it improves it etc.
Rabbi Elazar said: The Sages said,
All 'Truthfully they said' – it is a halakha.

steps.triggerLanguage: בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ: בְּיַיִן הִתִּירוּ לְעָרֵב קָשֶׁה בְּרַךְ, מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהוּא מַשְׁבִּיחוֹ.

rashi.hebrew: מפני שמשביחו - קשה משביח את הרך לפיכך פסק עמו רך מערב בו קשה ודוקא קתני קשה ברך ולא רך בקשה פסק עמו קשה לא יערב בו את הרך וכן אין צריך לומר חדשים בישנים דוקא אבל ישנים בחדשים שפיר דמי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that mixing strong wine with weak wine is permitted because the strong wine enhances the weak, but the reverse is forbidden as the weak wine would not enhance the strong.

rashi.hebrew: דאין מערבין - חדשות בישנות דהואיל ובחזקת ישנות מוכרן נמצא מאנהו ומוכר לו זולות בתורת יקרות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that mixing new and old grain is forbidden because the seller is selling cheaper new grain under the pretense of it being more expensive old grain.

rashi.hebrew: מפני שאדם רוצה לישנן - מפני שעילוי דמיהן של חדשות אינן מפני שהן טובות כישנות אלא שאדם רוצה לישנן וזה שפסק עמו על הישנות אינו רוצה לישנן לפיכך אסור לערב בהו חדשות ואפי' מעולות בדמים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the prohibition against mixing new and old grain applies even when the new grain is more expensive, because the buyer specifically wants old grain to age it further, and the seller does not share that intent.

rashi.hebrew: באמת הלכ' - מדיהב טעם למילתי' מפני שמשביחו ואין לגמגם בדבר ונקט בה למתניתין באמת ש"מ כל היכא דתני באמת הלכה ואין לחסם ולגמגם בדבר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase 'בֶּאֱמֶת אָמְרוּ' indicates a settled הלכה without dispute because the Mishnah provides a clear reason ('מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהוּא מַשְׁבִּיחוֹ'), leaving no room for further questioning.

steps.title: Rav Naḥman's limitation: only between the winepresses
steps.stepSummary: Rav Naḥman limits the permission to mix wine to the period when the wine is still among the winepresses.
steps.whatsHappening: Rav Naḥman restricts the Mishnah's permission to a specific time frame.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Rav Naḥman clarifies that the permission to mix strong and weak wine applies only 'בֵּין הַגִּתּוֹת' — when the wine is still in the process of fermentation among the winepresses. At this stage, mixing enhances the flavor. After fermentation, mixing would harm the wine.
steps.whatToRemember: The permission to mix wine is limited to the fermentation period.
steps.confusionAlert: This limitation seems to contradict the Mishnah's general statement, which doesn't mention a time restriction.
steps.whyThisMatters: This sets up the Gemara's question about how mixing is permitted today, after the fermentation period.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַב נַחְמָן: וּבֵין הַגִּיתּוֹת שָׁנוּ.
phrases.english: Rav Naḥman says: And between the winepresses they taught.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב נַחְמָן: וּבֵין הַגִּתּוֹת שָׁנוּ.

rashi.hebrew: ובין הגיתות שנו - דמשביחו שתוססין זה עם זה ונעשים טעם אחד אבל לאחר הגיתות שכבר קלט כל אחד ריחו וטעמו אין משביחו אלא פוגמו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah's permission to mix wines applies only בֵּין הַגִּתּוֹת because at that stage the fermenting wines blend to create a unified, improved flavor, whereas after fermentation each wine has fixed its own taste and mixing would only damage it.

steps.title: Challenge to Rav Naḥman's Limitation
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how mixing wine is permitted today, when it is no longer between the winepresses.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara challenges Rav Naḥman's limitation by asking how mixing is permitted today. This is a direct challenge to the practical application of the Mishnah's rule.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Gemara presses Rav Naḥman's limitation: if mixing is only permitted during fermentation, how can people mix wine today, when it is no longer 'בֵּין הַגִּתּוֹת'? This forces the Gemara to find a justification for contemporary mixing practices.

phrases.aramaic: וְהָאִידָּנָא דְּקָא מְעָרְבִי שֶׁלֹּא בֵּין הַגִּיתּוֹת?
phrases.english: And today, when they are mixing not between the winepresses?

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַב פָּפָּא: דְּיָדְעִי וְקָא מָחֲלִי.
phrases.english: Rav Pappa said: They know and they are waiving.

phrases.aramaic: רַב אַחָא בְּרֵיהּ דְּרַב אִיקָא אָמַר: הָא מַנִּי?
phrases.english: Rav Aḥa, son of Rav Ika, said: This is whose?
It is Rabbi Aḥa, as it is taught:
Rabbi Aḥa permits in a matter that is tasted.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְהָאִידָּנָא דְּקָא מְעָרְבִי שֶׁלֹּא בֵּין הַגִּתּוֹת?

rashi.hebrew: דידעי - הכל יודעים שהוחזקו לערב:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רב פפא's answer relies on the premise that the public is aware of the mixing and therefore can be considered to have waived their rights.

rashi.hebrew: בדבר הנטעם - שאדם טועם קודם שלקחו ויכול להבחין שנתערב בו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that רבי אחא's leniency regarding דבר הנטעם applies when a person tastes the wine before purchasing it, allowing them to detect any mixture.

steps.title: Internal Contradiction Regarding Dregs
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the Mishnah's statement about dregs, noting a contradiction between the prohibition on mixing dregs and the permission to sell wine with its dregs.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara identifies a contradiction within the Mishnah's statements about dregs. It notes that the Mishnah first prohibits mixing dregs with wine, but then permits selling wine with its dregs.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Gemara rejects the suggestion that the permission to sell with dregs implies informing the buyer, because the latter part of the Mishnah explicitly discusses informing the buyer, implying that the earlier part does not. This forces the Gemara to find a resolution to the apparent contradiction.

phrases.english: And one does not mix wine sediments into wine,

phrases.aramaic: אֲבָל נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֶת שְׁמָרָיו וְכוּ׳.
phrases.english: but one gives him his sediments etc.

phrases.aramaic: וְהָא אָמְרַתְּ רֵישָׁא אֵין מְעָרְבִין כְּלָל!
phrases.english: And yet you said in the beginning: one does not mix at all!

phrases.aramaic: וְכִי תֵּימָא מַאי ״נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֶת שְׁמָרָיו״?
phrases.english: And lest you say: what is 'gives him his sediments'?
That he informs him,
Here from the latter clause it is taught:

phrases.aramaic: לֹא יִמְכְּרֶנּוּ בַּחֲנוּת אֶלָּא אִם כֵּן מוֹדִיעוֹ,
phrases.english: One should not sell it in the store unless he informs him,

phrases.aramaic: וְלֹא לַתַּגָּר אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁמּוֹדִיעוֹ,
phrases.english: And not to a merchant even though he informs him,

phrases.aramaic: מִכְּלָל דְּרֵישָׁא אַף עַל גַּב דְּלָא מוֹדַע לֵיהּ!
phrases.english: From the general rule of the beginning: even though he does not inform him!

steps.triggerLanguage: וְאֵין מְעָרְבִין שִׁמְרֵי יַיִן בְּיַיִן, אֲבָל נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֶת שְׁמָרָיו וְכוּ׳. וְהָא אָמַרְתָּ רֵישָׁא אֵין מְעָרְבִין כְּלָל!

rashi.hebrew: והא מדקתני סיפא - ומפליג בין הודיעו ללא הודיעו מכלל דרישא דקתני אבל נותן לו את שמריו אע"ג דלא אודעיה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's rejection of the suggestion that 'נותן לו את שמריו' implies informing the buyer is based on the fact that the latter part of the Mishnah explicitly distinguishes between informing and not informing, implying that the earlier part refers to a case where the seller does not inform the buyer.

tosafot.hebrew: [מאי] (אבל) נותן לו שמריו דקמודע ליה - דס"ד דאפ"ה אסור שלא ירמה בו אחרים:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות clarifies that the Gemara's initial assumption ('דקא מודע ליה') was that the seller informs the buyer, but even so, it would be prohibited because the seller might deceive others with the wine.

steps.title: Rav Yehuda's Resolution on Dregs
steps.stepSummary: Rav Yehuda resolves the contradiction by stating that the prohibition is on mixing dregs from different days, not on selling wine with its own dregs.
steps.whatsHappening: Rav Yehuda offers a resolution to the Gemara's challenge. He clarifies that the prohibition is on mixing dregs from different days.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Rav Yehuda resolves the contradiction by clarifying that the prohibition is on mixing dregs from different days (e.g., yesterday's dregs with today's wine, or vice versa). However, one may sell wine with its own dregs (i.e., dregs from the same day). This is supported by a baraita in the name of Rabbi Yehuda, which explicitly states this distinction.
steps.whatToRemember: The prohibition is on mixing dregs from different days, not on selling wine with its own dregs.

phrases.english: Rav Yehuda said: Thus he says,

phrases.aramaic: אֵין מְעָרְבִין שְׁמָרִים שֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ בְּשֶׁל יוֹם,
phrases.english: One does not mix sediments of yesterday with those of today,
and not those of today with those of yesterday.
But he gives him his sediments.
It is also taught thus,
Rabbi Yehuda says:
One who pours wine for his friend –

phrases.aramaic: הֲרֵי זֶה לֹא יְעָרֵב שֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ בְּשֶׁל יוֹם,
phrases.english: this one does not mix yesterday's with today's,
and not today's with yesterday's.

phrases.aramaic: אֲבָל מְעָרֵב שֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ בְּשֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ,
phrases.english: But he mixes yesterday's with yesterday's,
and today's with today's.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב יְהוּדָה: הָכִי קָאָמַר, אֵין מְעָרְבִין שְׁמָרִים שֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ בְּשֶׁל יוֹם, וְלֹא שֶׁל יוֹם בְּשֶׁל אֶמֶשׁ. אֲבָל נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֶת שְׁמָרָיו.

rashi.hebrew: של אמש - מיין ששפה אמש ונשארו השמרים אין מערבין אותו ביין ששפה היום ששמרי יין זה מקלקלין יין אחר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that mixing dregs from yesterday's wine with today's wine is prohibited because the dregs of one wine spoil the other.

rashi.hebrew: אבל נותן הוא לו את שמריו - של יין עצמו ויום ואמש לאו דוקא והוא הדין ליום ויום משתי חביות אלא אורחא דמילתא נקט דסתם יום ואמש משתי חביות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the distinction between 'yesterday' and 'today' is not strict, but rather a common way to refer to dregs from two different barrels, and one may sell wine with its own dregs.

rashi.hebrew: השופה יין - כל דבר הצלול הנזרק בנחת מכלי אל כלי שלא יתערבו בו שמרים קרו לה שפייה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י defines שפייה as the careful pouring of clear liquid from one vessel to another to avoid mixing in the dregs.

steps.title: Abaye's Challenge to Rava's Practice
steps.stepSummary: Abaye challenges Rava's practice of selling diluted wine to a merchant, citing the Mishnah's prohibition.
steps.whatsHappening: Abaye challenges Rava's practice based on the Mishnah's prohibition. The Gemara relates an incident where Rava bought wine from a store, diluted it with water, found it unpalatable, and sent it back to the store to be sold.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Abaye challenges this practice, citing the Mishnah's prohibition on selling diluted wine to a merchant, even if the merchant is informed. Abaye is pressing on the practical application of the Mishnah's rule. This forces Rava to justify his practice.

phrases.english: One who had water mixed with his wine,
this one may not sell it in the store,
unless he informs him, etc.
Rava brought him wine from the store,
He poured it, tasted it, it was not good,
He sent it to the store. Abaye said to him:
And yet we learned, and not to a merchant,
even though he informed him!

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: מִזְגָּא דִּידִי מִידָּע יְדִיעַ.
phrases.english: He said to him: My pouring is known to all.

phrases.aramaic: וְכִי תֵּימָא דְּטָפֵי וּמְחַיְּילֵיהּ וּמְזַבֵּין לֵיהּ –
phrases.english: And if you say that he adds drops and strengthens it and sells it to him –
If so, there is no end to the matter.

steps.triggerLanguage: רָבָא אַיְיתוֹ לֵיהּ חַמְרָא מֵחָנוּתָא, מַזְגֵיהּ, טַעְמֵיהּ, לָא הֲוָה בְּסִים, שַׁדְרֵיהּ לְחָנוּתָא. אֲמַר לֵיהּ אַבָּיֵי: וְהָא אֲנַן תְּנַן, וְלֹא לַתַּגָּר, אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁהוֹדִיעוֹ!

rashi.hebrew: והא אנן תנן ולא לתגר שאינו אלא לרמות כו' - וחנווני היינו תגר ויחזור חנווני זה וימכרנו בחזקת יין בחנות והרי המים מעורבין בו ואתה מכרת לו ואיכא לפני עור (ויקרא יט):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבא's action violates the Mishnah because the storekeeper is a תגר who will sell the diluted wine as pure wine, thereby causing the customer to transgress לפני עור.

rashi.hebrew: מזיגא דידי מידע ידיע - שאני נותן בו מים הרבה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבא's defense, מזיגא דידי מידע ידיע, means he added so much water that the dilution is obvious and known to everyone.

rashi.hebrew: וכי תימא מייתי חמרא חייא ומערב ביה - עד שלא יהא ניכר טעם המים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's concern, וכי תימא דטפי ומחייליה ומזבין ליה, is that the storekeeper might add more wine to the mixture to mask the taste of the water before selling it.

rashi.hebrew: אין לדבר סוף - שאף המים לבדם אסור למכור לחנווני שמא יערבם ביין ולא חשו אלא בזמן שאני מוכר לו דבר העשוי לרמות בו כמות שהוא עכשיו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that אין לדבר סוף means we only prohibit selling something that is currently ready to deceive, but not selling pure water which the storekeeper might later mix with wine.

tosafot.hebrew: רבא אייתו ליה חמרא - רבא גרסינן דאשכחן שהיתה מזיגתו משונה כדאמרינן בנדרים (דף נה.) ובכיצד מעברין (עירובין דף נד.) דמיא האי מזיגא למזגא דרבא בריה דרב יוסף בר (חייא) והשתא ניחא הא דאמר רבא בפרק המוציא יין (שבת דף עז. ושם ד"ה דאמר) כל חמרא דלא דרי על חד תלת מיא לאו חמרא הוא ובפרק אחד דיני ממונות (סנהדרין לז.) אמרינן אל יחסר המזג שאם הוצרכו מהם לצאת אם יש שם כ"ג כנגד סנהדרי קטנה יוצאים ואם לאו אין יוצאים משמע דמזיגה על חד תרי דקרא [איירי] במזיגת סתם בני אדם ומיהו קשה דמשמע התם דס"ל לרבא דעיקר מזיגה כך דינה דמוקי מתני' דבמזוג ב' חלקים מים ואחד יין ביין השרוני דוקא דרפי ונראה דרבא מוקי מזיגה דקרא דאל יחסר המזג ביין השרוני והרב מנחם מיוני פירש דאל יחסר המזג שלא יחסרו נ' מסנהדרי גדולה דמזג בגימ' נ' אבל אם יחסר מ"ט ישארו כ"ב ואין ב"ד שקול מוסיפין עליהם אחד הרי כ"ג ומיהו בירושלמי משמע דאיירי במזיגת יין ממש:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that רבא's unusual dilution ratio is supported by his statements in שבת and סנהדרין, where he defines standard dilution as one part wine to two parts water.

steps.title: The Rule of Local Custom and Rav's Limitation
steps.stepSummary: The Mishnah permits diluting wine according to local custom, and Rav limits this permission to the period between the winepresses.
steps.whatsHappening: The Mishnah permits diluting wine according to local custom, and Rav limits this permission. The Mishnah permits diluting wine according to local custom, specifying that one may dilute by half, a third, or a quarter.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Rav then limits this permission to the period 'בֵּין הַגִּתּוֹת', when the wine is still fermenting. After fermentation, dilution would cause the wine to spoil. This reinforces Rav Naḥman's earlier limitation and clarifies the scope of the permission to dilute wine.

keyTerms.meaning: a place where they are accustomed
to add water to the wine

steps.whatToRemember: The permission to dilute wine is limited to the fermentation period.

phrases.aramaic: מָקוֹם שֶׁנָּהֲגוּ לְהַטִּיל מַיִם בַּיַּיִן, יָטִילוּ וְכוּ׳.
phrases.english: A place where they are accustomed to pour water into the wine, they pour, etc.
A Tanna taught: To half, to a third, and to a quarter.
Rav said: And between the winepresses they taught.
phrases.notes: Refers to the period when wine is still in the winepresses, before fermentation.

steps.triggerLanguage: מָקוֹם שֶׁנָּהֲגוּ לְהַטִּיל מַיִם בַּיַּיִן, יָטִילוּ וְכוּ׳. תָּנָא: לְמֶחֱצָה, לִשְׁלִישׁ, וְלִרְבִיעַ. אָמַר רַב: וּבֵין הַגִּתּוֹת שָׁנוּ.

rashi.hebrew: למחצה לשליש ולרביע - הכל כמנהג המדינה יטיל מים אם מחצה מחצה אם שליש שליש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah's mention of diluting by half, a third, or a quarter means that the permissible amount of water depends entirely on the local custom of that place.

steps.title: The Mishna on Perkus and Price Reduction
steps.stepSummary: The Mishna presents Rabbi Yehuda's prohibition on giving nuts to children and reducing prices, contrasted with the Chachamim's permission.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya opens with the Mishna's text regarding deceptive business practices. The Mishna establishes two prohibitions by Rabbi Yehuda: giving nuts to children to attract them, and reducing prices below market rate.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Chachamim permit both, with the latter being praised as 'זָכוּר לַטּוֹב'. This establishes the baseline halachic dispute on commercial ethics and deceptive marketing.

phrases.english: MISHNA: Rabbi Yehuda says:

phrases.aramaic: לֹא יְחַלֵּק הַחֶנְוָנִי קְלָיוֹת וֶאֱגוֹזִין לַתִּינוֹקוֹת,
phrases.english: A storekeeper may not distribute toasted grain and nuts to children,

phrases.aramaic: מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהוּא מַרְגִּילָן לָבֹא אֶצְלוֹ. וַחֲכָמִים מַתִּירִין.
phrases.english: because he accustoms them to come to him. And the Sages permit.

phrases.aramaic: וְלֹא יִפְחוֹת אֶת הַשַּׁעַר, וַחֲכָמִים אוֹמְרִים: זָכוּר לַטּוֹב.
phrases.english: And he may not reduce the price, and the Sages say: He is remembered for good.

steps.triggerLanguage: מַתְנִי׳ רַבִּי יְהוּדָה אוֹמֵר: לֹא יְחַלֵּק הַחֶנְוָנִי קְלָיוֹת וֶאֱגוֹזִין לַתִּינוֹקוֹת...

rashi.hebrew: מתני' ולא יפחות את השער - למכור בזול מפני שמרגיל לבא אצלו ומקפח מזונות חבירו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the prohibition against reducing the price (פחות את השער) is because it habituates customers to buy from him, thereby depriving his fellow merchants of their livelihood (מקפח מזונות חבירו).

rashi.hebrew: זכור לטוב - שמתוך כך אוצרי פירות מוכרין בזול:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Chachamim's praise 'זכור לטוב' refers to the benefit that grain stores (אוצרי פירות) are able to sell their goods at a lower price due to this practice.

steps.title: The Mishna on Sifting and Adornment
steps.stepSummary: The Mishna continues with Abba Shaul's prohibition on sifting beans and the general prohibition on adorning people, animals, or vessels for sale.
steps.whatsHappening: The Mishna text continues, introducing further prohibitions on deceptive practices. Abba Shaul prohibits sifting beans to remove waste, while the Chachamim permit it but agree that sifting only the top layer is deceptive.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Mishna concludes with a blanket prohibition on 'פרקוס' for people, animals, and vessels. This sets the stage for the Gemara to define 'פרקוס' and explore the boundaries of permissible commercial enhancement.

phrases.aramaic: לֹא יָבוֹר אֶת הַגְּרִיסִין, דִּבְרֵי אַבָּא שָׁאוּל.
phrases.english: One may not sift the ground beans, the words of Abba Shaul.

phrases.aramaic: וַחֲכָמִים מַתִּירִין, וּמוֹדִים שֶׁלֹּא יָבוֹר מֵעַל פִּי מְגוּרָה,
phrases.english: And the Rabbis permit, and they concede that one may not sift from the mouth of the bin,
for it is nothing other than deceiving the eye.
One does not adorn not a person,
and not an animal,
and not vessels.

steps.triggerLanguage: לֹא יָבוֹר אֶת הַגְּרִיסִין, דִּבְרֵי אַבָּא שָׁאוּל...

rashi.hebrew: גריסין - פולין גרוסות בריחים אחת לשתים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that גריסין refers to beans that have been ground in a mill.

rashi.hebrew: לא יבור את הפסולת - לפי שמתוך שנראות יפות הוא מעלה על דמיהם הרבה מדמי הפסולת שנטל מהם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that Abba Shaul prohibits sifting out the waste because the beans appear more attractive, leading the seller to inflate their price beyond the value of the waste removed.

rashi.hebrew: ומודים שלא יבור מעל פי המגורה - למעלה להראות יפות ואת הפסולת שבתוכו לא בירר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Chachamim agree that sifting only the top layer of the pit is prohibited because it makes the beans look good while leaving the waste inside.

rashi.hebrew: לפי שאינו אלא כגונב את העין - בברירה זו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that this specific act of sifting only the top layer is considered גונב את העין, or deceiving the eye.

rashi.hebrew: לא את האדם - עבד כנעני העומד לימכר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the prohibition on פרקוס for a person refers to a Canaanite slave who is being sold.

steps.title: Inquiry into the Chachamim's Reasoning
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks for the rationale behind the Chachamim's permission to give nuts to children.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara inquires about the Chachamim's reasoning. The Gemara seeks to understand why the Chachamim permit a practice that seems to create unfair competition by accustoming children to one store.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This is a neutral inquiry, not an objection. Understanding the Chachamim's rationale is key to defining the limits of permissible marketing.

phrases.english: GEMARA: What is the reason of the Rabbis?

phrases.aramaic: דַּאֲמַר לֵיהּ: אֲנָא מְפַלֵּיגְנָא אַמְגּוֹזֵי, וְאַתְּ פַּלֵּיג שִׁיסְקֵי.
phrases.english: Because he says to him: I distribute nuts, and you distribute jujubes.

rashi.hebrew: גמ' שיסקי - פרונ"ש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Aramaic term שִׁיסְקֵי is a loanword meaning 'nuts' (specifically hazelnuts), clarifying the Gemara's terminology.

steps.title: What is the חֲכָמִים's reason for praising price reduction?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks why the Chachamim praise reducing prices below market rate.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks why the חֲכָמִים praise lowering prices. This is a setup line seeking the rationale behind their approval.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The inquiry probes the ethical basis for praising price cuts. It challenges the assumption that lowering prices might harm market stability.

phrases.english: And one may not reduce the price
And the Sages say: He should be remembered for good etc.
What is the reason of the Sages?

steps.triggerLanguage: וְלֹא יִפְחוֹת אֶת הַשַּׁעַר, וַחֲכָמִים אוֹמְרִים: זָכוּר לַטּוֹב וְכוּ׳. מַאי טַעְמָא דְּרַבָּנַן?

rashi.hebrew: מאי טעמא - נקט זכור לטוב לשון ברכה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Chachamim use the phrase זכור לטוב as a form of blessing to express their approval of price reduction.
